full_prompt: Design one representative elevated single-family coastal home pile foundation + lateral force resisting system for a Zone V (coastal high hazard area) site, fully compliant with FEMA P-499 Home Builder's Guide to Coastal Construction (2019 reprint). The structure above is a two-story wood-frame home with 10 m x 12 m (~120 m2) footprint elevated to a bottom-of-lowest-horizontal-structural-member elevation at the design flood elevation (DFE) plus freeboard, supported on a 4x5 grid (20 total) of Class C 250x250 mm (10x10 in nominal) southern yellow pine timber piles or equivalent square RC piles driven into sandy coastal soil. The site is on the Gulf Coast at ASCE 7 ultimate design wind speed Vult = 160 mph (71.5 m/s) 3-second gust (within the P-499 130-180 mph coastal band), Exposure D, Risk Category II. Each pile must develop its required vertical bearing and tension (uplift) capacity from embedment per the P-499 pile embedment tables for sandy soil and provide lateral capacity against the combined wind + wave + current loading of a design-event hurricane with breaking waves at the stillwater depth ds = 1.2 m (4 ft) per FEMA P-499 V-zone wave loading equations (Fbrkp = 1.1 Cp gamma_w ds^2 per unit width on vertical piles, plus impulsive Cp coefficient). The continuous vertical load path (roof sheathing -> rafter-to-wall connection -> wall stud -> wall-to-floor -> floor-to-pile cap -> pile anchorage) must be closed using Simpson Strong-Tie or equivalent hurricane connectors whose rated uplift capacities per P-499 connector tables exceed the ASCE 7 factored uplift demand with a safety factor >= 1.0 at every joint. Deliverable is a single assembled CAD model of the pile grid, pile caps, floor framing, LFRS (lateral force resisting system) shear-wall layout, and connector locations (represented as placed components), sized so the model can run linear static FEA for wind + wave and a connector demand/capacity check. Vibration, thermal, fluid CFD, radiation, and buckling analyses are NOT required; waves are applied as equivalent static loads per FEMA equations; pile lateral capacity uses p-y non-FEA geotech solver.
short_prompt:
Design the pile foundation + continuous vertical load-path for a two-story wood-frame single-family coastal home on a Zone V (coastal high hazard) Gulf Coast site, per FEMA P-499 Home Builder's Guide to Coastal Construction (2019 reprint). Structural system is a 4x5 grid (20 total) of Class C timber (or equivalent square RC) piles driven into sandy soil; the home is elevated to DFE + freeboard, and hurricane connectors carry the uplift load path from roof through studs, floor, pile caps and pile anchorage.

Envelope: 10 x 12 m (~120 m^2) footprint, 250 x 250 mm (10x10 in) piles at 3.0 m spacing, elevated >= 0.6 m above BFE.

Load cases.
- LC1 design wind: ASCE 7 Vult 160 mph 3-sec gust, Exposure D (within the P-499 130-180 mph coastal band).
- LC2 V-zone breaking wave: stillwater depth ds = 1.2 m, Fbrkp = 1.1 Cp gamma_w ds^2 per unit width on vertical piles and on the bottom of the lowest horizontal structural member.
- LC3 dead + live: tributary gravity loads from the two-story wood frame.
- LC4 uplift: ASCE 7 0.6D + W combination along the continuous load path; every connector joint must meet demand/capacity >= 1.0 vs. tested manufacturer tables.

Pile lateral capacity is sized with a p-y geotech analysis; connector capacities come from tested tables.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Lateral pile capacity in sand is a soil-structure interaction problem solved with p-y curves (Reese, API), not standard structural FEA.
Simpson Strong-Tie / USP connector capacities come from published tested load tables, not FEA.

prompt.title: FEMA P-499 Zone V elevated pile foundation for coastal home
prompt.brief: Design a 4x5 pile foundation + load path for a 10 x 12 m two-story coastal home at Vult = 160 mph with V-zone 1.2 m breaking waves and continuous connector load path.

material.piles: southern yellow pine (or equivalent square RC) Class C timber pile
material.connectors: galvanized steel hurricane connectors per Simpson Strong-Tie / equivalent tested tables

load_cases.name: breaking_wave_Vzone
load_cases.equation: Fbrkp = 1.1 Cp gamma_w ds^2 per unit width (FEMA P-499)
load_cases.application: horizontal force on each pile and on bottom of lowest structural member

load_cases.name: uplift
load_cases.application: ASCE 7 0.6D + W wind uplift combination along load path

boundary_conditions.pile_tips: fixed bearing in sand per p-y analysis output
boundary_conditions.ground_elevation: stillwater level and scour per P-499

objective.secondary: minimize pile embedment depth within allowable

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: factored demand per ASCE 7 vs capacity from P-499 embedment table
p-y analysis vs combined wind + wave + current shear
Simpson / USP tested allowable uplift vs ASCE 7 0.6D + W

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam (piles, floor framing), shell (diaphragms), spring (connectors + soil)

exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Wave loads are reduced to equivalent static per P-499 equations; no CFD.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Pile buckling is envelope-checked by embedment tables; not a governing separate analysis here.

eval_coverage: coupled structural FEA + p-y geotech non-FEA solver
